title: CSI IL / DL — Insert / Delete Line
summary: IL (final 'L') inserts Pn blank lines at the cursor row, scrolling lines below downward within the scroll region; DL (final 'M') deletes Pn lines at the cursor row, scrolling lines below upward. Both default Pn to 1 and operate only within the current scroll region (DECSTBM).

attribution.note: xterm ctlseqs: CSI Ps L = Insert Ps Lines (IL); CSI Ps M = Delete Ps Lines (DL). Default Ps = 1.
ECMA-48 defines IL (final 'L') and DL (final 'M') operating within the scroll region, default Pn 1.

frame.note: CSI + single Pn + final byte: 'L' insert-line (0x4C), 'M' delete-line (0x4D). Pn defaults to 1; scoped to the scroll region.

terminator_detail.note: No string terminator; the CSI sequence ends at its final byte. Parameters Ps are decimal, ';' (0x3B) separated.

params.name: IL — Insert Line (L)
params.meaning: CSI Pn L inserts Pn blank lines at the cursor row; existing lines from the cursor down scroll downward within the scroll region. Blank lines take the current background color.

params.name: DL — Delete Line (M)
params.meaning: CSI Pn M deletes Pn lines starting at the cursor row; lines below scroll upward and blank lines appear at the bottom of the scroll region.

gotchas: IL/DL operate within the current scroll region (DECSTBM); lines outside the region are not affected and do not move.
DL's final byte 'M' (0x4D) is unrelated to the mouse-report 'CSI M'; context (parameters/prefix) disambiguates.
Inserted/blank lines are filled with the current SGR background color.
If the cursor is outside the vertical scroll region, IL/DL have no effect.

v1_smoke_test.asserts: IL ('L') and DL ('M') render as byte-exact single-parameter CSI sequences.
